description_long: This is a weathered, military-style jet airplane with a camouflage paint scheme blending greens, browns, and grays. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the rear, with a single engine intake at the nose and a jet exhaust at the tail. The wings are swept back and mounted low on the fuselage, showing signs of wear and possible battle damage, with some parts appearing bent or broken. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, also swept back and showing visible distress. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a canopy that blends into the body. The landing gear is partially visible and appears to be of the tricycle type, with the main gear under the wings and a nose wheel under the cockpit.
description: A weathered, camouflaged military jet airplane with swept-back low wings, a single vertical tail, and a forward enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the nose intake, forward cockpit canopy, and the left wing with visible camouflage and wear.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the jet exhaust, swept tail, and the left wing's trailing edge damage.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the right wing, tail section, and the overall tapering fuselage.
description_view_3: Right-rear view focusing on the tail assembly, right wing, and the distressed surface details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and slight anhedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and swept horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose wheel, partially visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single-seat.
